meta.purpose: Audit-trail acknowledgments only. No Guide re-review required. EA proceeds to item 2.4 immediately after emission.

acknowledgments_verbatim.ack: I will inspect tools/fleet_observability/critical_notify.ps1 at item 2.5 and confirm the WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md Appendix A.1 audit finding (BlarAI reference in comment only, no runtime path). If confirmed: document the no-action disposition with explicit Appendix A.1 citation in the closing handoff. If a runtime BlarAI path is discovered: STOP and report to Lead Architect before any refactor or scope expansion.

The same inspection rule applies to any other PS1 the expanded 3-directory audit scope (tools/scheduled-tasks/, tools/fleet_ops/, tools/fleet_observability/) surfaces beyond the canonical 7: default = no-action with documented citation; runtime-path discovery = STOP and report. Never silent scope expansion mid-stage.

I will begin item 2.4 (Python entrypoints argparse refactor) work after emitting these acknowledgments. The 16 pre-existing binding acknowledgments + 7 Guide-#4 acknowledgments + these 2 critical_notify-watchpoint acknowledgments remain in force through Stage 4 cutover (per i1-2 dedup rule).

closing_handoff_commitments_recap.item: Decision on legacy tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py: route through new chokepoint OR fold in entirely
critical_notify.ps1 inspection result with Appendix A.1 citation

standing_reminders_active.r: Fleet stays paused throughout (verify state.json on each substantive edit; never call state.resume_fleet during Stage 2)
If context exhaustion threatens before item 2.9: emit v3 mid-stage handoff XML (docs-only commit), then stop. No partial-commit refactor.

ea_acknowledgments_proceed.next_action: Beginning item 2.4 — Python entrypoints argparse refactor. First action: read tools/_project_context.py and tools/_vikunja_client.py to lock the helper APIs, then audit each of the 12 enumerated Python entrypoints + tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py.
